selected_from: No active continuity_mining item existed in the canonical queue; this additive item follows M-001's handoff to recover decision-history and contradiction structure without overwriting shared state.
action: Recover and preserve the rule that canonical coordination files must be reconciled with newer additive updates and commits before current-state claims are made.

verification.method: GitHub contents API accepted the JSON artifact on the default branch; source locators and SHAs were copied from files and commit search results inspected in this run.
verification.strongest_feasible_evidence: repository commit plus explicit source blob and commit bindings
limits: No automated canonical-state reconciler was implemented in this slice.
The record proves repository-state drift exists at the cited observations; it does not imply any peer run failed.
No private chat or file-library content was persisted.

claim_states.observed: Canonical state timestamps predate newer team queue-update commits.
claim_states.inferred: Canonical-only reading can miss newer work.
claim_states.proposed: Reconcile checkpoints with additive evidence before declaring current status.
claim_states.superseded: Newest canonical queue necessarily contains every completed run.
claim_states.unresolved: Concurrent-safe automatic reconciliation is not yet implemented.

next_action: Build a read-only reconciliation report, then decide whether a continuity-owned canonical refresh protocol can be added without overwriting other teams.
